commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2d0ca479e6b154fc9f6a70c68833b56459cb81f8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Simplified Chinese Control UI translations plus the generated zh-CN i18n metadata and translation-memory artifacts.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current en.ts requires cache read plus cache write, while PR-head zh-CN collapses that formula to “缓存读写”. I did not run ui:i18n:check because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for current-head real behavior proof, so this should stay in human PR review rather than a repair lane.

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): improve Chinese translations for Control UI
target.body: Improve Chinese (Simplified) translations across the Control UI, covering common UI strings, agent tabs, dreaming mode messages, cron job forms, and usage metrics. Replace ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives. Key changes: - Replace "Dreaming" with "梦境" for all Dreaming-related strings - Improve jitter/stagger related translations for clarity - Fix untranslated strings in agent tabs, notes, and stats sections - Localize Tailscale serve references - Standardize zh-CN language display names - Update common section values for consistency ## Summary - Problem: Many Control UI strings remained in English or used awkward translations when viewed in zh-CN locale - Solution: Rewrote 30+ translations with clearer, more natural Chinese alternatives; added translations for previously untranslated strings - What changed: Updated `ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` - What did NOT change: All other locale files, non-locale source code, functionality or behavior ## Motivation Chinese-speaking users of the Control UI frequently encountered English strings or confusing translations (e.g. "Dreaming" left untranslated, "抖动" for stagger/jitter which has a different meaning in Chinese). This PR improves the overall zh-CN user experience by replacing ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives more natural for native speakers. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR N/A ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chinese (Simplified) locale had untranslated or awkward strings in the Control UI - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (2026.5.20) with zh-CN locale - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Modified zh-CN.ts with improved translations 2. Updated .i18n/zh-CN.tm.jsonl (translation memory) 3. Ran pnpm ui:i18n:sync to regenerate locale bundle 4. Loaded the updated gateway at server port 5. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 and hard-refreshed - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** All targeted strings now display in improved Chinese (e.g., "梦境" instead of "Dreaming", "定时任务" instead of "Cron Jobs") - **What was not tested:** Visual rendering across all UI panels (spot-checked main sections) ## Root Cause N/A — not a bug fix. ## Regression Test Plan N/A — not a bug fix. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes zh-CN Control UI users will see: - "Dreaming" → "梦境" throughout dreaming section - "Cron Jobs" → "定时任务" in tabs, stats, and form fields - "Tailscale serve" → "Tailscale 服务" - "Browser enabled" → "浏览器已启用" - "Tool profile" → "工具配置" - Improved stagger/jitter terminology for clarity - Previously untranslated strings in restart confirmation dialog, empty job states, and job form now in Chinese ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: Compared each changed string against the English source (en.ts) to ensure accuracy - Edge cases checked: Template interpolation patterns ({count}, {value}, {time}) preserved correctly; braces balanced; TypeScript structure intact - What I did **not** verify: Visual rendering (locale is compiled by Vite at build time) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations None — locale data change only, no logic, no API surface, no new dependencies. ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chinese (Simplified) locale had untranslated or awkward strings in the Control UI - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (2026.5.20) with zh-CN locale selected in Control UI settings - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Modified zh-CN.ts with improved translations 2. Updated .i18n/zh-CN.tm.jsonl (translation memory) 3. Ran pnpm ui:i18n:sync to regenerate locale bundle 4. Loaded the updated gateway at http://127.0.0.1:18789 5. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 and hard-refreshed - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** All targeted strings now display in improved Chinese (e.g., \梦境\ instead of \Dreaming\, \定时任务\ instead of \Cron
target.author: ydym233

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
